away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: DiJonai Carrington
players.injury_comment: Carrington underwent left foot surgery in April and there's been no indication that she's close to making her season debut.

players.name: Rickea Jackson
players.injury_comment: The season-ending knee injury is a devastating development for Jackson and the Sky, as the 25-year-old forward was well on her way to a career-best campaign. Through Chicago's first four games, Jackson averaged 18.0 points, 4.8 rebounds, 2.0 assists, 1.8 blocks and 1.5 three-pointers in 29.5 minutes per contest. After Jackson's early exit in Sunday's contest, Aicha Coulibaly opened the second half with the starters and looks to be the most likely candidate to move into the top unit for the Sky's next game Wednesday versus the Wings.

players.name: Gabriela Jaquez
players.injury_comment: Jaquez is looking to return from a four-game absence due to a left knee injury, but her official status may not be known until much closer to Sunday's 3:00 p.m. ET tipoff. Rachel Banham should continue to see more playing time for as long as Jaquez is out of action.

players.name: Courtney Vandersloot
players.injury_comment: Vandersloot is still recovering from the right knee surgery she underwent last June to repair a torn ACL, and there's been no indication that she's close to a return.

injuries.name: Gabriela Jaquez
injuries.comment: Jaquez is looking to return from a four-game absence due to a left knee injury, but her official status may not be known until much closer to Sunday's 3:00 p.m. ET tipoff. Rachel Banham should continue to see more playing time for as long as Jaquez is out of action.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Temi Fagbenle
players.injury_comment: Fagbenle will miss her 10th consecutive game due to a right shoulder sprain, with her lone appearance this season coming in the May 8 season opener against the Mystics. The center's next chance to return will come Wednesday against the Sun.

players.name: Kiki Rice
players.injury_comment: The absence doesn't come as much of a surprise, given that she was diagnosed with a Grade 2 left ankle sprain that she sustained during Wednesday's loss against the Liberty. Her next chance to play is this coming Wednesday against the Sun, though a clear recovery timeline has yet to be announced by the Tempo. Julie Allemand and Kia Nurse should both see an uptick in playing time for as long as Rice is out of action.
